questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow

Parking reserved for disabled permit holders

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow

Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction

Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed

Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median

Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

An intersection is ahead

The road ahead is divided by a median

Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

Two lanes are merging into one

choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

A right curve with a side road joining ahead

A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down

Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane

An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead

An intersection is ahead

Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road

The road curves right, then left

Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to make a full stop and wait until the signal turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light mean?
choices: Treat it like a stop sign: stop, then proceed when safe
Drive through without stopping
The signal is broken; ignore it
Yield only to pedestrians
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign, so you must come to a complete stop before proceeding.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
Stop and stay stopped
The intersection is closed
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red, so you should stop if it is safe rather than try to race through.

questions.prompt: In Alaska, when may you turn right at a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, unless a sign prohibits the turn
Never; right on red is illegal
Only when an arrow is green
Without stopping, if no police are present
questions.explanation: Alaska allows a right turn on red after you stop completely and yield, unless a posted sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
choices: Eight-sided (octagon)
Triangle
Diamond
Round
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon, a shape reserved only for stopping.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
choices: Yield the right-of-way
Stop completely
No passing

questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: What color are most construction and work-zone signs?

Blue
Green
Brown
questions.explanation: Orange signs warn of construction and maintenance work zones ahead.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a full stop
The road is closed

questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light warns you to reduce speed and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead
Mark a speed limit

questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards such as curves, intersections, or animal crossings.

questions.prompt: When a police officer is directing traffic at an intersection with a working signal, you should:
choices: Follow the officer's directions even if they differ from the signal
Obey only the traffic signal
Stop and wait for the signal to change
Choose whichever is more convenient
questions.explanation: Directions from a police officer always override traffic signals and signs.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The driver who arrives and stops first
The largest vehicle
The driver going straight
The fastest driver
questions.explanation: At a four-way stop, the first vehicle to arrive and stop proceeds first.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, who yields?
choices: The driver on the left yields to the driver on the right
The driver on the right yields to the driver on the left
The slower driver yields
Neither driver has to yield
questions.explanation: When two vehicles arrive simultaneously, the one on the left must yield to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic and crossing pedestrians
Proceed before oncoming traffic
Sound your horn and turn
Turn only on a red light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a private driveway or alley, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the roadway
Proceed because you have the right-of-way
Honk and merge
Stop only if a sign is posted
questions.explanation: Drivers entering from a driveway, alley, or private road must yield to vehicles and pedestrians already on the road.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal?
choices: At least 100 feet before turning
Only as you begin the turn
Two car lengths before turning
Signaling is optional
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: When you approach a pedestrian in a crosswalk, you should:
choices: Stop and yield to the pedestrian
Proceed if you can fit past them
Honk so they hurry
Speed up to clear the crosswalk
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: A left turn should generally begin from:
choices: The lane nearest the center line
The far right lane
Any lane that is open
The shoulder of the road
questions.explanation: Left turns are made from the lane closest to the center line so you don't cross other traffic.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by moving to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Before making a right turn, you should position your vehicle:
choices: As close to the right curb as practical
In the center of the road
Near the center line
On the left side of the lane
questions.explanation: Turning from close to the right curb keeps you from swinging wide into other lanes.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where two cars approach at right angles, the general rule is:
choices: Yield to the vehicle on your right
Yield to the vehicle on your left
The vehicle going faster proceeds
Both stop and wait
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, yield to the driver approaching from your right.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in an Alaska residential district?
choices: 25 mph
15 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Alaska's default residential district speed limit is 25 mph unless signs indicate otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in an Alaska business district?
choices: 20 mph
30 mph
40 mph
10 mph
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in an Alaska business district is 20 mph unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit on open Alaska roads outside towns?
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
45 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: Alaska's default open-road speed limit is 55 mph unless signs post a different limit.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit in an alley in Alaska?
choices: 15 mph
25 mph
20 mph
10 mph
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in an alley in Alaska is 15 mph.

questions.prompt: The 'three-second rule' helps you:
choices: Keep a safe following distance behind the vehicle ahead
Decide when to change lanes
Time your turns at intersections
Know when to brake at a yellow light
questions.explanation: The three-second rule sets a minimum gap so you have time to react if the car ahead stops.

questions.prompt: On wet or icy Alaska roads, you should:
choices: Increase your following distance
Keep the same following distance
Decrease your following distance
Drive faster to maintain control
questions.explanation: Slippery roads lengthen stopping distance, so you need a larger gap to stop safely.

questions.prompt: Driving at a safe speed means:
choices: Adjusting to weather, traffic, and road conditions, never above the limit
Always driving the posted speed limit
Keeping up with the fastest traffic
Driving as fast as you feel comfortable
questions.explanation: A safe speed is one suited to conditions and never higher than the posted maximum.

questions.prompt: The highest posted speed limit you will generally find in Alaska is about:
choices: 65 mph
75 mph
55 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: The highest posted speed limit on Alaska highways is generally 65 mph.

questions.prompt: When approaching a curve, you should:
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Accelerate through the curve
Maintain highway speed
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps you keep control without braking suddenly mid-turn.

questions.prompt: Why is excessive speed dangerous?
choices: It reduces reaction time and increases crash force
It improves vehicle control
It shortens stopping distance
It has no effect on safety
questions.explanation: Higher speed leaves less time to react and makes any resulting crash more severe.

questions.prompt: You may NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
A broken yellow line is on your side
You are on a straight, clear road
The road has two lanes in each direction
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: In Alaska, if you are driving slowly and five or more vehicles are stacked up behind you, you must:
choices: Pull over where safe and let them pass
Speed up to the limit
Stay in your lane and keep your speed
Turn on your hazard lights and continue
questions.explanation: Alaska law requires slow drivers to pull over and let backed-up traffic pass when five or more vehicles are following.

questions.prompt: On most roads, you should drive in the:
choices: Right lane except when passing
Left lane at all times
Center of the road
Lane with the least traffic regardless of position
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass keeps traffic flowing and lets faster vehicles by.

questions.prompt: When is it safe to return to your lane after passing a vehicle?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
As soon as you pull alongside it
Whenever you feel like it
Only after slowing down
questions.explanation: Seeing the vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to return safely without cutting it off.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
Passing is never allowed
The lane is for parking
The road is one-way
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line permits passing on your side when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: You should NOT attempt to pass when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far ahead
On a long, straight, open road
Traffic ahead is moving slowly and clearly visible
The center line is broken
questions.explanation: Passing on hills or curves is dangerous because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check mirrors and blind spots
Just check your rearview mirror
Honk your horn
Speed up first
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking your blind spots ensures no vehicle is beside you before you move over.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Traffic moving in opposite directions
The road from the shoulder only
Parking spaces
questions.explanation: White lines divide lanes traveling the same way, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: When passing another vehicle, you should:
choices: Pass on the left and not exceed the speed limit
Pass on the right whenever possible
Exceed the speed limit to finish quickly
Pass within an intersection
questions.explanation: You normally pass on the left and must stay within the posted speed limit while doing so.

questions.prompt: On Alaska's two-lane highways, slow-vehicle turnouts are provided so that:
choices: Slower vehicles can pull aside and let faster traffic pass
Drivers can park overnight
Trucks can weigh in
Drivers can make U-turns
questions.explanation: Turnouts give slow-moving vehicles a safe place to let following traffic by.

questions.prompt: When you meet a stopped school bus with its red lights flashing, you must:
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Slow down and pass carefully
Honk and continue
Pass on the left only
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights on a school bus mean children are loading or unloading, so you must stop.

questions.prompt: A major wildlife hazard for Alaska drivers, especially at dawn, dusk, and in winter, is:
choices: Moose entering the roadway
Falling rocks
Tumbleweeds
Stray dogs only
questions.explanation: Moose collisions are common in Alaska, so scan the roadsides and slow down in posted areas.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with large trucks, you should:
choices: Avoid lingering in their blind spots
Cut in front and brake to slow them down
Drive close behind to draft
Pass on the right as they turn
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots, so stay where the driver can see you and never cut in and brake.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them plenty of room
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to move them off the road
Speed up beside them
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are legitimate road users who need a safe buffer of space when you pass.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles easy to miss in traffic?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
They are always larger than cars
They have brighter lights than cars
They never travel on highways
questions.explanation: Because motorcycles are small, you must look carefully and check blind spots before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When a large truck is making a wide right turn, you should:
choices: Not try to squeeze between the truck and the curb
Pass on its right side
Move up beside its front wheels
Follow closely through the turn
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn, so the space between the truck and the curb can be a deadly trap.

questions.prompt: If an animal suddenly enters the road, you should:
choices: Brake firmly and stay in your lane if possible
Swerve sharply into oncoming traffic
Speed up to pass it
Close your eyes and hope
questions.explanation: Braking while keeping control is usually safer than swerving, which can cause a worse crash.

questions.prompt: Pedestrians at an intersection have the right-of-way:
choices: In marked and unmarked crosswalks
Only at signals with a walk light
Only in marked crosswalks
Never; vehicles always go first
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians crossing in both marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: In Alaska, an adult driver is legally impaired (DUI) at a blood alcohol concentration of:
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.12 percent or higher
questions.explanation: A BAC of 0.08 percent or more is the legal limit for adult drivers in Alaska.

questions.prompt: Under Alaska's zero-tolerance law, a driver under age 21 can be charged for driving with:
choices: Any measurable amount of alcohol
0.08 percent BAC or more
0.05 percent BAC or more
Only after three drinks
questions.explanation: Alaska has zero tolerance for underage drinking and driving, so any detectable alcohol is a violation for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Alaska is:
choices: Illegal and a primary offense for all drivers
Allowed at red lights
Legal for adults
Allowed below 25 mph
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is banned for everyone in Alaska, and an officer can stop you for it alone.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a commercial driver in Alaska?
choices: 0.04 percent
0.08 percent
0.02 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter 0.04 percent BAC limit.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It improves your focus
It sharpens your vision
It has no effect at low amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol reduce coordination, reaction time, and judgment.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid impaired driving is to:
choices: Not drive after using alcohol or impairing drugs
Drink coffee before driving
Drive slowly after drinking
Roll down the windows for fresh air
questions.explanation: Only time removes impairment, so the safe choice is to arrange a sober ride.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medications
Only illegal drugs
Only alcohol
Nothing but lack of sleep
questions.explanation: Many legal medicines can cause drowsiness or impairment, so check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes:
choices: Texting, eating, or anything that takes attention off the road
Only using a handheld phone
Only adjusting the radio
Nothing if you keep one hand on the wheel
questions.explanation: Any activity that diverts your attention from driving is a dangerous distraction.

questions.prompt: If you plan to drink at an event, the best approach is to:
choices: Arrange a designated driver or another sober ride in advance
Wait an hour and then drive
Drive only on back roads
Eat a big meal and drive
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride ahead of time guarantees you won't drive impaired.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid on ice, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer where you want the front to go
Slam on the brakes
Accelerate hard
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: Steering gently in your intended direction and easing off the gas helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: In fog or falling snow, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog and snow, while high beams reflect back and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: Where signs say headlights must be on at all times in Alaska, you should:
choices: Keep your headlights on day and night
Use them only at night
Use parking lights instead
Ignore the sign in daylight
questions.explanation: Some Alaska roadways require headlights on at all times to improve visibility in low light and poor weather.

questions.prompt: Black ice is most likely to form on:
choices: Bridges and shaded areas
Sunny straightaways
Gravel pull-offs only
Dry pavement
questions.explanation: Bridges and shaded spots freeze first, so approach them with extra caution in cold weather.

questions.prompt: Before driving in winter, you should:
choices: Clear all snow and ice from windows and lights
Clear only the driver's side window
Wait for the snow to blow off as you drive
Leave the windows frosted to save time
questions.explanation: Full visibility and visible lights are essential for safe winter driving.

questions.prompt: When driving on snow or ice, you should:
choices: Slow down and brake gently
Brake hard to test traction
Keep your normal speed
Follow closely to stay in tracks
questions.explanation: Gentle inputs and lower speed help prevent skidding on slippery surfaces.

questions.prompt: For remote winter travel in Alaska, it is wise to:
choices: Carry an emergency kit and keep your fuel tank full
Travel with an empty trunk
Let the fuel run low to save weight
Leave the cell phone at home
questions.explanation: Long distances between services make an emergency kit and full tank important for safety.

questions.prompt: When visibility is reduced by rain, fog, or twilight, you should:
choices: Turn on your headlights and slow down
Use high beams in fog
Turn off your lights to reduce glare
Speed up to clear the area
questions.explanation: Headlights help you see and be seen, and lower speed gives you more time to react.

questions.prompt: Everyone in a vehicle in Alaska must:
choices: Wear a seat belt
Wear a seat belt only in the front seat
Wear a seat belt only on highways
Buckle up only if under 18
questions.explanation: Alaska requires all occupants to wear seat belts, and it is a primary offense to be stopped for.

questions.prompt: Which of these is an illegal place to park?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
In a marked parking stall
In a public parking lot
Along an unrestricted curb
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a fire hydrant blocks emergency access and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: In Alaska, a child under 8 years old who is under 65 pounds and shorter than 4 feet 9 inches must ride in:
choices: An appropriate car seat or booster seat
A regular seat belt only
The front seat with an adult
Any seat without restraint
questions.explanation: Alaska requires car seats or boosters for children under 8 who are under 65 pounds and shorter than 4 feet 9 inches.

questions.prompt: Failing to wear a seat belt in Alaska is:
choices: A primary offense you can be stopped for
A secondary offense only
Not enforced
Legal for adults
questions.explanation: Officers can stop and ticket you in Alaska solely for not wearing a seat belt.

questions.prompt: You should NOT park:
choices: In a crosswalk or intersection
In a marked space
Along an open curb
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk or intersection blocks pedestrians and traffic and is illegal.

questions.prompt: When you park on any hill, you should also:
choices: Set the parking brake
Leave the car in neutral
Leave the engine running
Turn off your lights only
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake helps keep the vehicle from rolling on a slope.

questions.prompt: A rear-facing child restraint is required for:
choices: Infants under 1 year of age
All children up to age 8
Children over 4 feet 9 inches
Children weighing over 65 pounds
questions.explanation: Alaska requires the youngest and smallest children, such as infants under 1 year, to ride in rear-facing seats.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit in Alaska?
choices: 14 years old
15 years old
16 years old
18 years old
questions.explanation: Alaska allows teens to apply for an instruction permit at age 14 after passing the knowledge test.

questions.prompt: While driving on an Alaska instruction permit, you must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
choices: 21 years old
18 years old
25 years old
16 years old
questions.explanation: A permit holder must always have a licensed driver age 21 or older in the vehicle.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice are required before advancing from a permit in Alaska?
choices: At least 40 hours, including 10 in challenging conditions
At least 20 hours
At least 60 hours
No specific number of hours
questions.explanation: Alaska requires 40 hours of supervised practice, with 10 of those in darkness, bad weather, or highway driving.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Alaska knowledge (written) test?
choices: 20 questions
25 questions
40 questions
50 questions
questions.explanation: The Alaska knowledge test consists of 20 questions.

questions.prompt: What score do you need to pass the Alaska knowledge test?
choices: 80 percent
70 percent
60 percent
90 percent
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: Before advancing to a provisional license, you must hold your instruction permit for at least:
choices: 6 months with no traffic convictions
1 month
3 months
12 months
questions.explanation: Alaska requires holding the permit for 6 months with a clean record before moving up.

questions.prompt: A provisional license holder in Alaska generally may NOT drive between:
choices: 1 a.m. and 5 a.m. except to or from work
Midnight and 6 a.m. for any reason
10 p.m. and 4 a.m.
9 p.m. and 5 a.m.
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers face a nighttime restriction from 1 a.m. to 5 a.m. unless driving to or from a job.

questions.prompt: A provisional license holder in Alaska generally may not carry passengers under 21 unless:
choices: They are siblings or a parent or guardian is present
They sit in the back seat
It is during the daytime
The trip is under 10 miles
questions.explanation: Young passengers are restricted for provisional drivers except for siblings or when a parent or guardian rides along.

questions.prompt: Which document is the official source for Alaska driving rules studied for the permit test?
choices: The Alaska Driver Manual
A national driving guide
The vehicle owner's manual
A private practice-test website
questions.explanation: The Alaska Driver Manual published by the DMV is the official study source for the knowledge test.

questions.prompt: If you are stopped by a police officer, you should:
choices: Pull over safely to the right and keep your hands visible
Speed up to a well-lit area first
Get out of your vehicle immediately
Refuse to stop until you reach home
questions.explanation: Pulling over promptly and keeping your hands visible keeps the stop safe for everyone.

questions.prompt: A conviction for driving under the influence in Alaska can result in:
choices: License revocation, fines, and possible jail time
A warning with no record
Only a small parking fee
No consequences for a first offense
questions.explanation: Even a first DUI in Alaska carries serious penalties including license loss, fines, and possible jail.

questions.prompt: When stopped by law enforcement, you must be able to show:
choices: Your license, registration, and proof of insurance
Only your license
Just your registration
Nothing unless arrested
questions.explanation: Drivers must provide their license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance on request.

questions.prompt: Traffic violations on your record can lead to:
choices: Points, fines, and possible license suspension
Free driving lessons
A discount on insurance
No effect at all
questions.explanation: Accumulating violations adds points and can result in fines and suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: Alaska's move-over requirement for stopped emergency vehicles means you should:
choices: Slow down and move over when safe
Maintain your speed and stay in your lane
Speed up to pass quickly
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: You must slow down and move over for stopped emergency and maintenance vehicles to protect workers.

questions.prompt: Because seat belt and texting laws are primary offenses in Alaska, an officer can:
choices: Stop and cite you for those violations alone
Only warn you
Cite you only with another violation
Never enforce them
questions.explanation: Primary offenses allow officers to stop and ticket you for that violation by itself.

questions.prompt: Driving in Alaska without required insurance can result in:
choices: Penalties and possible license consequences
A reward for saving money
No penalty if you drive carefully
Only a verbal warning forever
questions.explanation: Operating a vehicle without required insurance is a violation that carries penalties.

questions.prompt: A provisional driver who violates the nighttime or passenger restriction may face:
choices: A fine and points on their driving record
An automatic full license
Extra driving privileges
No consequences
questions.explanation: Breaking provisional restrictions results in a fine and points, which can delay full licensing.
